pi_items.source: install_qblade.sh installs QBlade v0.96.2 which uses .wpa format; setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/Documents/projects directory; task.json specifies this path

pi_items.verified_value: NACA 4421 and NACA 4412 are real airfoils documented in wind turbine research; QBlade project files contain plaintext airfoil names; blade definitions are stored in .wpa files
pi_items.source: Web search: airfoil specifications confirmed in academic research (ResearchGate, IJIRSET); QBlade documentation (docs.qblade.org) confirms project files store airfoil names and blade definitions as plaintext
Web search for small-scale HAWT specifications. NREL 15-kW reference turbine has 4.7m blades, confirming 5m is realistic for small-scale residential applications

No specific QBlade v0.96 project file size specifications found in documentation or web sources. 2048 bytes is plausible for a minimal project file but cannot be independently confirmed without an actual reference file.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses QBlade v0.96 (as specified in install_qblade.sh line 67), which natively supports .wpa project file format. NACA 4421 and NACA 4412 are real airfoils documented in wind turbine aerodynamic research. A 5-meter blade length is typical for small-scale horizontal-axis wind turbines (comparable to NREL 15-kW reference turbine at 4.7m). QBlade project files store airfoil names and blade definitions as plaintext, supporting the required_strings verification strategy. The expected output path and directory structure are properly set up by the task scripts.
